acceptanceSignals: Audit query supports filtering by actor, action, entity, and time.
Cross-flow correlation IDs link order, verification, review, and settlement events.
Audit retention and purge jobs follow policy and are test-verified.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Audit Trail and Investigation Query in 0 attempts

lastCommit.message: feat: implement Audit Trail and Investigation Query (attempt 4)

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The current feature satisfies the active policy: new audit HTTP routes are mirrored in the OpenAPI contract and generated TS client artifacts, route-to-spec parity and error-schema mapping checks are present, and the implementation includes immutable audit evidence, actor/action/entity/time investigation filters, cross-flow correlation, and retention/purge with committee-admin investigation capabilities.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: Static review indicates this feature satisfies the active policy: immutable audit evidence is emitted on high-risk write paths with locked actor context, investigation queries support actor/action/entity/time (plus correlation), committee-admin responsibility attribution and retention-purge flows are implemented, and retention/purge behavior is covered by dedicated tests without legacy/fallback audit paths in the current feature area.

excludedApproaches: Anonymous actor records for high-risk operations.
Using only transient service logs as audit evidence.
executionConstraints: All high-risk writes must emit immutable audit events with actor identity from locked auth model.
Audit storage/query lifecycle must follow defined retention/deletion policy.
Provide committee-admin investigation queries for responsibility attribution.
name: Audit Trail and Investigation Query
rationale: Governance, compliance, and dispute handling require durable accountability records.
summary: Implement immutable audit evidence and admin investigation capabilities.
